confidence.label: moderate

Score of 3 reflects partial corrigibility: present in the War Powers Resolution and Carter-era crisis acknowledgment, but contested and partially reversed in the Reagan era. reviewNote: Score value 3/5 defensible: memory-driven-restraint mechanism (WPR § 2(a) purpose clause, Carter crisis speech) represents partial corrigibility — present in the legislative and rhetorical record but contested and partially reversed in the Reagan era. Confidence 0.62 is well-calibrated given the periodization uncertainty. Chain expansion resolved (Milestone 7 added §5(b) and Weinberger passages). confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Score of 3 (moderate) for the 1973–1984 post-Vietnam period: the sacred political order retained democratic institutional coherence but lost Cold War consensus, executive war authority, and public confidence (per Carter). Mid-range score reflects the order's ambiguous status — neither collapsed (Soviet 1991) nor highly constrained (postwar Germany) nor strongly expansive (imperial Japan). Confidence at 0.60 because periodization is genuinely contested: Reagan-era rearmament suggests sacred-political-order strength recovered more than a 3 implies by the mid-1980s.

reviewNote: Score value 3/5 defensible: sacred-political-order-strength = 3 for the 1973-1984 post-Vietnam period captures the order's retained democratic coherence against its significant confidence and legitimacy erosion. Confidence 0.60 reflects periodization uncertainty (Reagan rearmament suggests faster recovery than a 3 implies). Hold at score-review; periodization should be explicitly bounded to 1973-1984 before human-reviewed.
